name: Caribou Chili
recipeIngredient: 1 cup red wine, beef broth, or beer
1 ½ cups canned tomatoes and juice
2 teaspoons liquid hot pepper sauce (or ½ teaspoon Cayenne Pepper)
recipeInstructions:
1. Place meat, onion, and garlic in a large heavy skillet or Dutch oven.
2. Cook until the meat is light brown.
3. Add oregano, cumin, water, chili powder, tomatoes, hot pepper sauce (to taste), and salt.
4. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for one hour.
5. Optionally, thicken the sauce with 1-2 tablespoons masa or cornmeal.
6. Allow the chili to cool completely before reheating and serving. It often tastes better the next day.
Optional ingredients:
1 cup chopped green bell pepper
1 cup chopped red bell pepper
Ancho, Pequin, Jalapeno, or other chile peppers
½ teaspoon allspice
2 tablespoons cilantro, fresh, chopped
1 teaspoon ground coriander
A small amount of red wine vinegar and an equal amount of brown sugar
Up to 2 teaspoons Worcestershire Sauce
Kidney beans (or any small red "chili" beans) – optional
